skill.name: product-changelog
skill.description: Create clear product changelogs, release notes, feature announcements, and visual update summaries for user-facing product communication.
skill.summary: Write user-focused changelogs and release notes with categories, visuals, and distribution guidance.
skill.author: inference-sh-9

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many markdown backticks, hardcoded URLs, and weak crypto indicators. Most are false positives from documentation examples, but the quick start includes a real curl-to-shell install pattern and networked CLI examples that warrant a publishing warning.

medium_findings.title: Remote Install Script Piped to Shell
medium_findings.description: The quick start runs a network download directly through the shell. This is a true positive because users execute remote content before manual inspection, even though the surrounding text describes checksum verification.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command uses a direct curl-to-shell pipeline. The file also offers verification notes, so this is risky documentation rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Networked CLI Commands in Skill Examples
medium_findings.description: The skill permits Bash access for infsh commands and includes examples that call external inference apps. This is expected for the skill purpose, but it can send prompts, URLs, or images to external services.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed tool and examples clearly invoke a networked CLI. No evidence of credential theft or hidden exfiltration was found.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The Ruby or shell backtick detections are false positives. The flagged locations are markdown fences, inline code, or examples shown to the user, not hidden runtime code in the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line review confirms these are markdown syntax markers or displayed examples. There is no Ruby code or hidden shell interpolation in these locations.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Documentation Links
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL findings are mostly documentation links to inference.sh resources or a placeholder user application URL. They do not show unauthorized network access by themselves.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible in user-facing documentation. The install URL is still risky when combined with shell execution, which is covered separately.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: No weak cryptographic algorithm use was found in the reviewed file. The only clear cryptography reference is a SHA-256 checksum note, which is not a weak algorithm for integrity checking.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the flagged lines found prose, examples, or tables rather than cryptographic implementation. No MD5, SHA-1, or insecure crypto call was present.

dangerous_patterns.title: Pipe to Shell Install Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The quick start uses a shell pipeline from a remote installer. Users should prefer manual download and checksum verification before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The exact dangerous pattern is present on one line. The adjacent install note lowers malicious-confidence but not execution-risk confidence.

content.value_statement: Product updates often read like internal commit logs and users miss important changes. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code write clear changelogs, release notes, and update announcements.
actual_capabilities: Turns product changes into user-facing changelog entries.
Classifies updates as new, improved, fixed, removed, or security.
Guides semantic versioning and date-based release formats.
Suggests visuals for UI changes, workflows, and performance updates.
Adapts release notes for changelog pages, email, in-app messages, and social posts.
Highlights breaking changes with timelines and migration steps.
limitations: It does not verify whether a product change actually shipped.
It needs accurate source notes from product, engineering, or support teams.
Visual generation examples depend on external inference.sh services.
It does not replace legal, compliance, or incident communications review.

use_cases.title: Weekly SaaS Changelog
use_cases.description: Convert a week of shipped changes into concise entries grouped by user impact.

use_cases.title: Breaking Change Notice
use_cases.description: Write a clear deprecation notice with dates, migration steps, and support paths.

use_cases.title: Launch Announcement Pack
use_cases.description: Create aligned copy for a changelog page, in-app message, and social update.

prompt_templates.title: Rewrite One Change
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for a single update.
prompt_templates.prompt: Rewrite this product change as a user-facing changelog entry. Include category, title, benefit, and one short explanation: [paste change].

prompt_templates.title: Create Release Notes
prompt_templates.scenario: Turn raw release notes into a structured update.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create release notes from these shipped changes. Group them into New, Improved, Fixed, Removed, and Security sections: [paste changes].

prompt_templates.title: Plan Visual Changelog
prompt_templates.scenario: Prepare visuals and distribution for a feature launch.
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft a feature announcement with recommended visuals, channels, and copy variants for changelog, email, and in-app notification: [feature details].

prompt_templates.title: Build Migration Notice
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a breaking change release note with what changed, who is affected, exact timeline, migration steps, risks, and support messaging: [details].

output_examples.input: A report export limit increased from 500 rows to 10,000 rows.
output_examples.output: New: Bulk Export for Reports. You can now export up to 10,000 rows from any report view.

output_examples.input: The dashboard query cache was refactored and large dashboards load faster.
output_examples.output: Improved: Faster Dashboard Loading. Dashboards now load faster when you filter large datasets.

output_examples.input: API v1 will stop working on March 15 and users must move to API v2.
output_examples.output: Breaking: API v1 Deprecation. API v1 stops working on March 15. Update integrations to API v2 before that date.

best_practices: Start each entry with the user outcome, not the implementation detail.
Group related changes and lead with the most important user benefit.
Use prominent timelines and migration steps for breaking changes.
anti_patterns: Publishing commit logs or ticket lists as release notes.
Using vague phrases such as bug fixes and improvements.
Burying breaking changes below routine updates.

faq.question: Can this skill write a full changelog page?
faq.answer: Yes. It can organize entries by date, category, and user impact.

faq.question: Does it support semantic versioning?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains major, minor, and patch version choices.

faq.question: Can it create social media announcements?
faq.answer: Yes. It can adapt a release note into short social or community posts.

faq.question: Does it generate product visuals?
faq.answer: It provides visual guidance and example inference.sh commands for screenshots and banners.

faq.question: Is it only for SaaS products?
faq.answer: No. It also supports APIs, mobile apps, and versioned software.

faq.question: What input should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide shipped changes, user impact, dates, audience, and any migration requirements.
